title: failed to start rootless container: "malloc" issues cause core dump of program in the container
body:
Given my infrastructure:
- bare metal server, 768GB RAM, 2 x CPU of 28 cores each (thus 56 in total), running Ubuntu 24.04LTS, kernel "6.8.0-31-generic"
- see below for the "podman info" output
- I have edited the file "/usr/share/containers/containers.conf" based on some suggestions that I found on the web, related to my issues:
```
[containers]
default_sysctls = [
  "net.ipv4.ping_group_range=0 0",
]
[engine]
cgroup_manager = "cgroupfs"
[machine]
cpus=2
memory=2048
```
- I have a set of BIND config files residing in "/store/central/dns/primary"
- trying to have rootless containers running
- starting this container is ok, but it exits immediately:
```
$ podman run -p 10053:53 --name my-bind9-container-prim --detach -e TZ=MET -v /store/central/dns/primary/cfg:/etc/bind docker.io/ubuntu/bind9:latest
7b4edb46358b751b48a38c79d839b50494853b1afb04cfb37d4ee837554a5faa
$ podman container ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                          COMMAND     CREATED        STATUS                      PORTS                  NAMES
7b4edb46358b  docker.io/ubuntu/bind9:latest              7 seconds ago  Exited (139) 6 seconds ago  0.0.0.0:10053->53/tcp  my-bind9-container-prim
```
- when I launch the container in interactive mode and try to launch the binary "/usr/sbin/named" from within the container, via "strace", I see that it spawns xxx threads, and encounters "malloc" failures:
```
$ podman run -it -p 10053:53 --name bind9-container-prim1 --detach -e TZ=MET -v /store/central/dns/primary/cfg:/etc/bind docker.io/ubuntu/bind9:latest /bin/bash
6879d9f1ac2d715e917d672d3878f44473990f7861c78005aba7687cafc2e569
$ podman container ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                          COMMAND     CREATED        STATUS        PORTS                  NAMES
c1521c1190fd  docker.io/ubuntu/bind9:latest  /bin/bash   2 minutes ago  Up 2 minutes  0.0.0.0:10053->53/tcp  bind9-container-prim
$ podman exec -it c1 /bin/bash
root@c1521c1190fd:/# dpkg -l|grep strace
root@c1521c1190fd:/# apt-get update >/dev/null && apt-get -y install strace >/dev/null 2>&1
root@c1521c1190fd:/# strace -f /usr/sbin/named -f -4 -u bind 2>&1 | tee /tmp/strace.out
...
[pid   263] futex(0x570526ce1268, FUTEX_WAKE_PRIVATE, 2147483647) = 0
[pid   263] mprotect(0x7efda00da000, 8192, PROT_READ|PROT_WRITE) = -1 ENOMEM (Cannot allocate memory)
[pid   263] mmap(0x7efb00000000, 67108864, PROT_NONE, MAP_PRIVATE|MAP_ANONYMOUS, -1, 0) = 0x7efaf8000000
[pid   263] mprotect(0x7efaf8000000, 143360, PROT_READ|PROT_WRITE) = -1 ENOMEM (Cannot allocate memory)
[pid   263] munmap(0x7efaf8000000, 67108864) = 0
[pid   263] mmap(NULL, 12288, PROT_READ|PROT_WRITE, MAP_PRIVATE|MAP_ANONYMOUS, -1, 0) = -1 ENOMEM (Cannot allocate memory)
...             
[pid   266] +++ killed by SIGABRT (core dumped) +++
[pid   265] +++ killed by SIGABRT (core dumped) +++
[pid   264] +++ killed by SIGABRT (core dumped) +++
[pid   263] +++ killed by SIGABRT (core dumped) +++
+++ killed by SIGABRT (core dumped) +++
``` 
The lines with "core dumped" occur for 114 different PIDs...

=> anyone has a clue what's causing this issue ?

PS: output from "podman info": 
